'''Josh Woods''' (born 13 December 1997) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|so}} for the [[Batley Bulldogs]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

==Background==
Woods was born in [[Leigh, Greater Manchester]], England.

==Career==
===Wigan Warriors===
He played for the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]], but spent the 2019 and 2020 seasons on loan at the [[Leigh Centurions]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://leighrl.co.uk/wp/v2/blog/2019/12/03/josh-woods-signs-up-for-second-season-on-loan-with-leigh/|title=Josh Woods Signs Up For Second Season On Loan With Leigh|website=www.leighrl.co.uk|date=3 Dec 2019|accessdate=19 Dec 2020}}</ref>

In 2017 he made his [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] [[Super League]] début against the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref name="Wigan Warriors v Salford Red Devils">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/ERLP3963917|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170506142734/http://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/ERLP3963917|url-status=dead|archive-date=6 May 2017|title=Wigan Warriors v Salford Red Devils|publisher=BBC|date=5 May 2017|accessdate=5 May 2017}}</ref>

===Newcastle Thunder===
On 21 December 2020 it was announced that Woods would join the [[Newcastle Thunder]] for the 2021 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55403530|title=Wigan Warriors: Josh Woods joins Newcastle Thunder while Jake Shorrocks is released|website=BBC Sport|date=21 Dec 2020|accessdate=22 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Batley Bulldogs===
On 4 November 2022 it was announced that Woods would join the [[Batley Bulldogs]] for the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/former-wigan-warriors-and-leigh-leopards-halfback-finds-new-club-for-2023-61619/?fbclid=IwAR3rQBcoh5IfFOVuvuYEc3_irMITilbaSI467RaqkH1kInSAzmouybI7DfY&amp|title=Former Wigan Warriors and Leigh Leopards halfback finds new club for 2023|website=Serious About RL|date=4 Nov 2022|accessdate=4 Nov 2022}}</ref>
